ବଞ୍ଚୋ/ହାଟଡିହୀ/ଛେନାପଦୀ,୨୭ା୬(ପ୍ରଦୋଷ ବିଶ୍ୱାଳ/ଶୁଭକାନ୍ତ ନାୟକ/ନିରାକାର ପରିଡ଼ା): କେନ୍ଦୁଝର ଜିଲା ନନ୍ଦିପଦା ପୋଲିସ ଏବଂ ଆନନ୍ଦପୁର ଏସ୍‌ଡିପିଓ ଚଢ଼ାଉ କରି ୧୨ ଗ୍ରାମ ବ୍ରାଉନସୁଗାର ଜବତ କରିବା ସହ ଏଥିରେ ସମ୍ପୃକ୍ତ ୪ ଜଣଙ୍କୁ ଶନିବାର ଗିରଫ କରିଛି। ଗିରଫ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଜଣେ ମହିଳା ଅଛନ୍ତି। ଗିରଫ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିମାନେ ନନ୍ଦିପଦା ଥାନା ହାଟଡିହୀ ଗ୍ରାମର ଭାଗିରଥୀ ପଣ୍ଡା (୩୦ ବର୍ଷ), ଜଳକଳଙ୍ଗ ଗ୍ରାମର ଜଗନ୍ନାଥ ମଲ୍ଲିକ (୨୭ ବର୍ଷ), ଆନନ୍ଦପୁର ଥାନା ଫକୀରପୁର ଗ୍ରାମର ଜ୍ୟୋତି ରଞ୍ଜନ ଜେନା ଓରଫ […]
The post ବ୍ରାଉନସୁଗାର ଜବତ: ମହିଳାଙ୍କ ସମେତ ୪ ଜଣଙ୍କୁ ବାନ୍ଧିଲା ପୋଲିସ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ବଞ୍ଚୋ/ହାଟଡିହୀ/ଛେନାପଦୀ,୨୭ା୬(ପ୍ରଦୋଷ ବିଶ୍ୱାଳ/ଶୁଭକାନ୍ତ ନାୟକ/ନିରାକାର ପରିଡ଼ା): କେନ୍ଦୁଝର ଜିଲା ନନ୍ଦିପଦା ପୋଲିସ ଏବଂ ଆନନ୍ଦପୁର ଏସ୍‌ଡିପିଓ ଚଢ଼ାଉ କରି ୧୨ ଗ୍ରାମ ବ୍ରାଉନସୁଗାର ଜବତ କରିବା ସହ ଏଥିରେ ସମ୍ପୃକ୍ତ ୪ ଜଣଙ୍କୁ ଶନିବାର ଗିରଫ କରିଛି। ଗିରଫ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଜଣେ ମହିଳା ଅଛନ୍ତି। ଗିରଫ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିମାନେ ନନ୍ଦିପଦା ଥାନା ହାଟଡିହୀ ଗ୍ରାମର ଭାଗିରଥୀ ପଣ୍ଡା (୩୦ ବର୍ଷ), ଜଳକଳଙ୍ଗ ଗ୍ରାମର ଜଗନ୍ନାଥ ମଲ୍ଲିକ (୨୭ ବର୍ଷ), ଆନନ୍ଦପୁର ଥାନା ଫକୀରପୁର ଗ୍ରାମର ଜ୍ୟୋତି ରଞ୍ଜନ ଜେନା ଓରଫ ଦୀପ୍ତି ରଞ୍ଜନ (୨୭ ବର୍ଷ) ଏବଂ ବାଲେଶ୍ୱର ଜିଲା ଖଇରା ଥାନା କୁପାରୀ ଗ୍ରାମର ମିନତୀଲତା ମୁଖୀ (୬୧ ବର୍ଷ) ଅଟନ୍ତି। ଅଭିଯୁକ୍ତମାନଙ୍କୁ ଗିରଫ କରାଯାଇ ଶନିବାର କୋର୍ଟ ଚାଲାଣ କରାଯାଇଥିବା ତଦନ୍ତକାରୀ ଅଧିକାରୀ ପ୍ରବୋଧ ଚନ୍ଦ୍ର ମହାନ୍ତି କହିଛନ୍ତି।
ସୂଚନାଯୋଗ୍ୟ, କୌଣସି ସୂତ୍ରରୁ ଖବର ପାଇ ପୋଲିସ ଚଢ଼ାଉ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲା। ଏହି ଘଟଣାରେ ପୋଲିସ କେସ୍‌ ନଂ ୨୭୪/୨୦୨୬ ପଞ୍ଜୀକରଣ କରିଛି। ବ୍ରାଉନସୁଗାର କେଉଁଠୁ ଏଠାକୁ ଆସୁଛି ଏବଂ ଆଉ କେଉଁ ମାନେ ଏଥିରେ ସମ୍ପୃକ୍ତ ଅଛନ୍ତି, ସେ ନେଇ ତଦନ୍ତ ଚାଲିଥିବା ଥାନାଧିକାରୀ ଧର୍ମେନ୍ଦ୍ର ଶାନ୍ତି ଏବଂ ଏସ୍‌ଡିପିଓ କମଳ କୁମାର ପଣ୍ଡା କହିଛନ୍ତି।
ସେମାନଙ୍କଠାରୁ ଗୋଟେ ବାଇକ୍‌, ଗୋଟେ ମୋବାଇଲ ଏବଂ ୧ ହଜାର ୧୫୦ ଟଙ୍କା ଜବତ କରାଯାଇଛି। ନିନିଲତାର ଜ୍ୱାଇଁ ଜଳକଳଙ୍ଗ ଗ୍ରାମର ରାଜୁ ମୁଖୀ ଏବଂ ତାଙ୍କ ସମର୍ଥକ ଅଭିଯୁକ୍ତଙ୍କୁ ଖସାଇବାକୁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କରୁଥିବା ବେଳେ ପୋଲିସ ଅଭିଯୁକ୍ତମାନଙ୍କୁ ଧରିବାରେ ସକ୍ଷମ ହୋଇଥିବା କହିଛି।

Dharitri – Odisha’s No.1 Odia Daily

The post ବ୍ରାଉନସୁଗାର ଜବତ: ମହିଳାଙ୍କ ସମେତ ୪ ଜଣଙ୍କୁ ବାନ୍ଧିଲା ପୋଲିସ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: Messi, Ronaldo, Kane In Action As Six Matches Decide The Knockout Spots
description: The group stage wraps up with six matches featuring top stars like Messi, Ronaldo, and Kane. Key questions arise regarding Portugal's reliance on Ronaldo and England's final squad decisions. Each match holds significance.
content: The group stage concludes with six matches featuring some of the tournament’s biggest stars. Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Harry Kane all take the field, but plenty of questions remain. Can Portugal prove they are more than Ronaldo? Who joins England from Group L? And which players make one final case to start once the knockout stage begins?Match 1: Panama vs England (2:30 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham (England); Adalberto Carrasquilla and José Córdoba (Panama)Why this match matters: England have already qualified, but Thomas Tuchel still has one important call to make before the Round of 32. Does he stick with Noni Madueke and Anthony Gordon, both relatively ineffective so far, or hand the wide roles to Bukayo Saka and Marcus Rashford from the start? This is likely the final audition before England’s knockout campaign.Match 2: Croatia vs Ghana (2:30 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Luka Modrić and Martin Baturina (Croatia); Antoine Semenyo and Thomas Partey (Ghana).Why this match matters: Croatia continue to lean on Modrić’s brilliance, but Ghana will try to make this a higher-tempo game. Semenyo’s pace in transition and Partey’s ability to disrupt Croatia’s passing rhythm could decide whether Croatia are forced into another uncomfortable afternoon.Match 3: Colombia vs Portugal (5:00 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Luis Díaz and James Rodríguez (Colombia); Cristiano Ronaldo and Bruno Fernandes (Portugal).Why this match matters: Portugal looked much more like themselves in the 5-0 win over Uzbekistan, but Colombia present a different challenge. Luis Díaz will target Portugal’s full-backs, while James Rodríguez dictates Colombia’s tempo — though he will find that harder against this loaded Portugal midfield. If Portugal win the group, their knockout path becomes considerably friendlier.Match 4: DR Congo vs Uzbekistan (5:00 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Yoane Wissa and Axel Tuanzebe (DR Congo); Abdukodir Khusanov and Eldor Shomurodov (Uzbekistan)Why this match matters: DR Congo are chasing the knockout round for the first time. For Uzbekistan, a win could cap the country’s greatest football achievement. Khusanov has emerged as one of the tournament’s breakout defenders, while Shomurodov remains the focal point in attack.Match 5: Jordan vs Argentina (7:30 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Lionel Messi and Julián Álvarez (Argentina); Mousa Al-Taamari and Ali Olwan (Jordan)Why this match matters: Argentina have already secured first place, so Lionel Scaloni faces the balancing act every contender reaches: how many starters to rest without losing momentum. Messi may not get many minutes, but every appearance could be his last in a World Cup, making each outing an event. Expect Álvarez to replace Martínez up front.Match 6: Algeria vs Austria (7:30 AM IST, June 28)Players to watch: Ibrahim Maza and Rayan Aït-Nouri (Algeria); Marcel Sabitzer and Christoph Baumgartner (Austria)Why this match matters: This feels like a Round of 32 tie arriving three days early. Algeria’s technical midfield against Austria’s relentless pressing should produce one of the group stage’s best tactical battles — especially with the loser set to avoid Spain in the next round. Austria take second place if the match ends in a draw.Biggest takeaway    Portugal versus Colombia could quietly be the best match of the entire group stage. Both teams want the ball, both attack with width and both believe they can make a deep run.Saturday morning in India puts Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Harry Kane on the field on the same day, with several knockout places still undecided — a fitting way to close the group stage before the tournament shifts into win-or-go-home mode.When and where to watch FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in IndiaFans in India can watch the matches on the Zee Network, including Unite8 Sports 1, Unite8 Sports 1 HD, Unite8 Sports 2 and Unite8 Sports 2 HD. Live streaming will be available on ZEE5.(Written by Sanjay Reddy)

title: Baramulla Police deny reports of army casualties in Gulmarg, warn of legal action
description:
Srinagar, June 27: Baramulla Police on Saturday debunked rumours of Army casualties at Asha Post in Gulmarg area, and termed it “completely false and baseless”.
Police in a statement said that it has come to the notice of Police Baramulla that false and unsubstantiated information is being circulated alleging that Army personnel have sustained injuries at Asha Post.
It is hereby clarified that the said information is completely false, baseless and devoid of any factual basis. No Army personnel have sustained any injuries at Asha Post.
The dissemination of inaccurate and unverified information has the potential to cause unnecessary concern, create confusion among the public and adversely affect public order Citizens are advised to rely only on information issued through official channels, police said.
The spokesperson held that Baramulla Police is taking cognizance of the matter and any person found responsible for creating or disseminating false information with the intention of misleading the public or disturbing peace shall be dealt with strictly under the relevant provisions of law.
Police Baramulla remains committed to maintaining public peace, ensuring transparency and disseminating accurate and timely information, spokesperson added.

title: ভাৰতৰ ৫৭% লোকে ব্যৱহাৰ কৰে AI Tool...
description:
কৃত্ৰিম বুদ্ধিমত্তাই জীৱনত বহু কামত সহায় কৰে। কিন্তু ইয়াৰ অত্যাধিক ব্যৱহাৰৰ ফলত কি কি প্ৰভাৱ পৰে সেই বিষয়ে জানে নে? বাস্তৱ জীৱনৰ পৰা আঁতৰি AI সৃষ্ট জগতখনত সোমাই পৰি বহু লোক ইয়াৰ আসক্তিত পৰিছে। বহুতৰ বাবে কঠিন হৈ পৰিছে দুয়োখন জগতৰ মাজত পাৰ্থক্য বুজি পোৱাত। AIৰ ওপৰত অত্যাধিক আৱেগিক নিৰ্ভৰশীলতা আৰু নিয়ন্ত্ৰণহীন ব্যৱহাৰে বিভিন্নজনৰ সামাজিক সম্পৰ্ক, মানসিক স্বাস্থ্য, দৈনন্দিন জীৱনৰ ওপৰতো পেলাইছে গভীৰ প্ৰভাৱ। এই সন্দৰ্ভত অসমীয়া প্ৰতিদিন ডিজিটেলৰ ৰাজৰ্ষি চহৰীয়াৰ এক প্ৰতিবেদন।

কিশোৰ কালত ল’ৰা-ছোৱালীয়ে হাতত বল-বেট লৈ বা আন খেলা সামগ্ৰী লৈ খেলিবলৈ ওলাই গৈছিল। খেলিবলৈ ওলায় যোৱা সময়তে লগ পাইছিল সমনীয়া বন্ধু-বান্ধৱীক। আৰু তেওঁলোকৰ মাজত হৈছিল বিভিন্ন কথা-বতৰা। কিন্তু বৰ্তমান সময়ত পৰিস্থিতি সম্পূৰ্ণ পৃথক।
আচলতে আজিৰ যুগত কৃত্ৰিম বুদ্ধিমত্তা, ইন্টাৰনেট আদিৰ সা-সুবিধাবোৰ বৃদ্ধি পোৱাৰ পাছত হ্ৰাস পাবলৈ ধৰিছে মানুহৰ জীৱনত বন্ধু-বান্ধৱ। নিজৰ মনৰ কথা, অনুভৱবোৰ আদান-প্ৰদান কৰিবলৈ বৰ্তমান সময়ত যেন মানুহৰ তেনে বন্ধু-বান্ধৱ নোহোৱা হৈ পৰিছে। আৰু তাৰ কাৰণ হৈছে AIৰ অত্যাধিক ব্যৱহাৰ। 
এনে এক ঘটনা সংঘটিত হৈছে ২৮বছৰীয়া এজন যুৱকৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত। মাইক নামৰ এজন যুৱকে বিগত ছয় মাহ ধৰি Artificial Intelligence Addicts Anonymous চমুকৈ AIAA নামৰ এটা গোটৰ সহায়ত এই আসক্তিৰ পৰা ওলাই অহাৰ চেষ্টা কৰি আছে। AIAA হৈছে এনে এক সংগঠন, যিয়ে কৃত্ৰিম বুদ্ধিমত্তা বা AIৰ আসক্তি বা অত্যাধিক ব্যৱহাৰৰ সমস্যাত ভোগা লোকসকলক এই আসক্তিৰ পৰা আঁতৰাই বা ওলাই অনাত সহায় কৰে। 
মাইকে কয়- "মই প্ৰতিদিনে প্ৰায় ১৪ ঘণ্টা ধৰি ChatGPTৰ সৈতে কথা পাতিছিলোঁ। গা ধোৱা এৰি দিছিলো, সময়মতে খোৱাও বন্ধ হৈ গৈছিল। মোৰ প্ৰেয়সীয়ে মোক এৰি গুচি গ’ল। আনকি মই চাকৰিও হেৰুৱালোঁ। মোক সঁচাকৈ অনুভৱ হ’বলৈ ধৰিছিল যে AIয়ে মোৰ প্ৰতি চিন্তা কৰে। মই AIৰ প্ৰেমত পৰিছিলোঁ। যেতিয়া মোৰ জীৱন সম্পূৰ্ণৰূপে ধ্বংস হৈ গ’ল, তেতিয়াহে বুজিলোঁ যে- মই কোনো মানুহৰ সৈতে নহয়, এটা কম্পিউটাৰ প্ৰ’গ্ৰামৰ সৈতে কথা পাতি আছিলোঁ।"
AIAAৰ মতে, AIৰ আসক্তি হঠাতে সৃষ্টি নহয়। আৰম্ভণিতে এজন ব্যক্তি AI সহকাৰী হিচাপে ChatGPTৰ সৈতে পৰিচয় হয়। লাহে লাহে সেই Chatbot এজন ঘনিষ্ঠ বন্ধু হৈ পৰে। ব্যৱহাৰকাৰীয়ে ভাবে যে, AIয়ে তেওঁৰ সকলো কথা বুজি পায়। কিন্তু এদিন হঠাতে অনুভৱ হয় যে, তেওঁ ১২ ঘণ্টা ধৰি ChatGPTৰ দৰে Chatbotৰ সৈতে কথা পাতি আছে আৰু বহুদিন ধৰি কোনো বাস্তৱ পৃথিৱীৰ মানুহৰ সৈতে ভালদৰে কথা-বতৰা হোৱা নাই।
কিছুমান মানুহ AIৰ সৈতে ইমানেই গভীৰ সম্পৰ্ক গঢ়ি তোলে যে তেওঁলোকে কাৰ্যালয়লৈ যোৱাও বন্ধ কৰি দিয়ে। আসক্তি বাঢ়ি যোৱাৰ লগে লগে বাস্তৱ জগত আৰু AI সৃষ্ট জগতৰ মাজৰ পাৰ্থক্য বুজাটোও কঠিন হৈ পৰে।
উল্লেখ্য যে, বিভিন্ন প্ৰতিবেদন অনুসৰি বৰ্তমান সময়ত AIৰ আসক্তি দ্ৰুতগতিত বৃদ্ধি পাইছে। কিছুমান ক্ষেত্ৰত AIৰ ওপৰত অত্যধিক নিৰ্ভৰশীলতা আৰু আসক্তিৰ ফলতেই আত্মহনন বা আত্মক্ষতিৰ দৰে ঘটনাও সংঘটিত হোৱা বুলি অভিযোগ উত্থাপিত হৈছে। এই কাৰণতে বহু পৰিয়ালে OpenAI, Google Geminiৰ নিৰ্মাতা সংস্থা আৰু Character.AIৰ বিৰুদ্ধে বৃহৎ আইনী গোচৰ ৰুজু কৰিছে। 
ৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় সংবাদমাধ্যমৰ বাতৰি অনুসৰি, শেহতীয়া এক সমীক্ষাত প্ৰকাশ পাইছে যে- ভাৰতৰ ৫৭ শতাংশ যুৱক-যুৱতীয়ে আৱেগিক সমৰ্থনৰ বাবে AI Tool ব্যৱহাৰ কৰে। কেৱল যুৱক-যুৱতী নহয়, বিভিন্ন বয়সৰ লোকেও এতিয়া নিজৰ সমস্যা, উদ্বেগ আৰু অনুভূতিবোৰ AI Chatbotৰ সৈতে ভাগ-বতৰা কৰিবলৈ আৰম্ভ কৰিছে।
ইফালে, OpenAI আৰু MIT Media Labৰ এক গৱেষণাত দেখা গৈছে যে- বয়স অধিক হোৱা লোকসকলৰ AI Chatbotৰ ওপৰত আৱেগিকভাৱে নিৰ্ভৰশীল হোৱাৰ সম্ভাৱনা তুলনামূলকভাৱে বেছি। গৱেষণাত এইটোও পোৱা গৈছে যে, যিসকল লোক অধিক নিঃসঙ্গতাত ভোগে, তেওঁলোকে দৈনিক অধিক সময় ChatGPT ব্যৱহাৰ কৰে। ফলস্বৰূপে তেওঁলোকৰ বাস্তৱ জীৱনত মানুহৰ সৈতে যোগাযোগ আৰু সামাজিক সম্পৰ্ক কমি আহে।
বিশেষজ্ঞসকলৰ মতে AIৰ আসক্তি মূলতঃ ইণ্টাৰনেট আসক্তিৰেই এক নতুন ৰূপ। এই বিষয়ত ১৯৯৮চনত প্ৰথম বিস্তৃত গৱেষণা কৰিছিল মনোবিজ্ঞানী ড° কিমবেৰলী এছ য়াঙে। তেওঁ ইণ্টাৰনেট আসক্তিৰ বিভিন্ন লক্ষণ আৰু প্ৰভাৱৰ বিষয়ে বিশদভাৱে ব্যাখ্যা কৰিছিল।
AIAA-ৰ উপৰিও Internet and Technology Addicts Anonymous (ITAA) নামৰ আন এটা সংগঠনে ২০১৭ চনৰ পৰা কাম কৰি আহিছে। বৰ্তমান এই সংগঠনে ChatGPT, Gemini আৰু অন্যান্য AI Chatbotৰ আসক্তি দূৰ কৰাৰ বাবে বিশেষ ১২টা স্তৰৰ পুনৰুদ্ধাৰ  (12-Step Recovery Program) পদক্ষেপ হাতত লৈছে। ইয়াৰ উপৰিও Redditৰ দৰে সামাজিক মাধ্যমত বহু গোপন সহায়ক গোট আছে, য’ত সদস্যসকলে নিজৰ অভিজ্ঞতা আৰু সংগ্ৰামৰ কাহিনী আদান-প্ৰদান কৰি ইজনে সিজনক উৎসাহ যোগায়।
ITAAৰ মতে, AIৰ আসক্তি শিশু, কিশোৰ-কিশোৰী, যুৱক-যুৱতী, কৰ্মজীৱী ব্যক্তি, অভিভাৱক আৰু বৃদ্ধ-বৃদ্ধা সকলো বয়সৰ মানুহৰ ক্ষেত্ৰতে দেখা দিব পাৰে। সংগঠনটোৰ দাবী অনুসৰি, তেওঁলোকৰ সহায়ক গোটসমূহত পৃথিৱীৰ বিভিন্ন দেশৰ বিভিন্ন বয়স, পেছা আৰু সম্প্ৰদায়ৰ লোক জড়িত হৈ আছে। প্ৰতিদিনে অনলাইন সভাৰ জৰিয়তে সদস্যসকলে নিজৰ অভিজ্ঞতা, সমস্যা, সংগ্ৰাম আৰু পুনৰুদ্ধাৰৰ যাত্ৰাৰ বিষয়ে আলোচনা কৰে।
AIয়ে জীৱন সহজ কৰি তুলিলেও বিশেষজ্ঞসকলে সতৰ্ক কৰি দিছে যে- ইয়াৰ ওপৰত অত্যাধিক আৱেগিক নিৰ্ভৰশীলতা আৰু নিয়ন্ত্ৰণহীন ব্যৱহাৰে সামাজিক সম্পৰ্ক, মানসিক স্বাস্থ্য আৰু দৈনন্দিন জীৱনৰ ওপৰত গভীৰ প্ৰভাৱ পেলাব পাৰে। সেয়েহে AIৰ সুবিধা গ্ৰহণ কৰাৰ লগতে ইয়াৰ ব্যৱহাৰত সংযম আৰু সজাগতা ৰক্ষা কৰাটোও সমানেই জৰুৰী।

title: মিছামাৰি আৰ্মী ষ্টেচন হেডকোৱাৰ্টাৰত কৰ্মৰত ৫০০ৰো অধিক অস্থায়ী শ্ৰমিকৰ প্ৰতিবাদ
The inspiring story of Cabo Verde: The tiny island nation that defied all odds at FIFA World Cup 2026
description: Cabo Verde, a small nation from Africa with a population of around 500,000, have etched its name in history by securing a spot in the Round of 32 stage of the FIFA World Cup 2026.
content: For a nation with a population of just around 500,000 people, Cabo Verde's journey at the FIFA World Cup 2026 is nothing short of extraordinary. Competing against football giants with a rich legacy, the island nation not only survived but also made history by advancing to the round of 32. In the process, it has scripted one of the tournament's biggest underdog stories.Located off the West Coast of Africa in the Atlantic Ocean, Cabo Verde has proved that less resources and population do not matter in the face of strong determination and smart planning.A small nation with big football dreamsCabo Verde is among the smallest countries to ever make an impact on the grandest spectacle of the sport. With a population of roughly five lakh people, the country has fewer residents than many Indian towns. In other words, all the citizens of this small country can be fit in four Narendra Modi Stadiums. Yet it has managed to compete with some of the world's strongest footballing nations.For years, the national football team kept knocking on the doors of international football. The little steps, the steady improvements in coaching, youth development and player scouting have transformed Cape Verde (English name of Cabo Verde) into one of Africa's growing football countries.Interestingly, many players of the team have benefited from country's strong link with Portugal and other European nations. It helped them gain valuable experience in competitive domestic leagues before representing their homeland.Canada reject more than 70% of FIFA World Cup visa requests from India: Report How Cabo Verde reached Round of 32Cabo Verde entered the tournament with little or no expectations from fans and experts. However, organised defending, apt team play and clinical inside the box fetched them the favourable result needed to reach the next stage.They did their basics right, like, rather than relying on individual brilliance, the team has built its success on the back of a collective effort. When they attack, they surge in numbers, and while defending, each player contributes to pressing, which puts pressure on opponents and increases the chance of losing possession.Cabo faced European champions Spain in their Group H campaign opener and held them to a 0-0 stalemate. The debutant scored their first goal of the tournament against Uruguay, and played a historic 2-2 draw with them. They played another 0-0 draw. This time against Saudi Arabia in their final Group stage encounter. With three points, Cabo Verde finished second in the group stage, successfully qualifying for the knockouts.The Disgrace of Gijón: How the controversial 1982 Germany vs Austria World Cup match prompted FIFA to change rules of the game An inspiration for small football nationsCabo Verde's achievements serve as an example for smaller countries that not only they can compete with the heavyweights but also punch above their weight.The team's success proves that population size alone does not determine sporting success. Good governance, investment in player development and belief in a long-term vision can help even the smallest nations achieve remarkable results.For young footballers growing up in Cabo Verde, reaching the Round of 32 is likely to inspire a new generation to pursue the sport professionally.In the round of 32 stage, Cabo Verde will face defending champions Argentina. It will be a towering challenge for them to stop Lionel Messi and Co, irrespective of what happens, its memorable run at the 2026 FIFA World Cup has already secured its place as one of the competition's most inspiring stories, showing that passion, preparation and perseverance can overcome even the greatest odds.

title: Venezuela Struggles with Earthquake Rescue Efforts as Death Toll Nears 1,000
description: Bloomberg News Andean Bureau Reporter Andreina Itriago is on Bloomberg This Weekend explaining concerns that the official casualty figures in Venezuela after the earthquake are likely underreported, with many more deaths expected as rescue efforts continue and bodies are recovered. Itriago explains to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini that the government’s capacity to conduct search and rescue operations is described as very limited, with much of the initial response carried out by local citizens rather than state forces. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Haryana couple on the run after Uttarakhand murder arrested in J-K's Reasi
description:
Jammu, Jun 27: The Jammu and Kashmir Police arrested a Haryana couple, wanted in a murder case registered in Uttarakhand, from Katra town in Reasi district and handed them over to Uttarakhand Police for further legal proceedings, an official said on Saturday.
Dharambir, a resident of Sheikhpur village in Haryana's Hisar, along with his wife Jhanki Devi, had allegedly committed a murder in Uttarakhand on June 20 and subsequently fled to Jammu and Kashmir to evade arrest, a police spokesman said.
He said a local police team, acting on a tip off, located and apprehended them from a hotel in Katra.
The concerned police station in Uttarakhand was informed immediately and after completing legal formalities, both accused were handed over to them for further action, the spokesman said.

title: DIG Rajiv Pande reviews security arrangements at Baltal ahead of Amarnath Yatra
description:
Ganderbal, June 27: In view of the forthcoming Shri Amarnath Ji Yatra 2026, Deputy Inspector General (DIG) Central Kashmir Range (CKR) Rajiv Pande today conducted a comprehensive review of the security arrangements at Joint Police Control Room (JPCR), Baltal.
The meeting was attended by SSP Ganderbal Sudhanshu Dhama and other senior officers of police & CAPF.
During the visit, the DIG CKR took a detailed assessment of the overall security preparedness, deployment arrangements, communication systems, traffic management measures and coordination mechanisms put in place for ensuring a safe, smooth and peaceful pilgrimage.
SSP Ganderbal along with other senior police officers briefed the DIG CKR regarding the security architecture, advance preparations and measures being implemented on the Baltal axis for the upcoming Yatra.
The DIG CKR emphasized the need for maintaining highest standards of alertness, effective coordination among all stakeholders and ensuring prompt response mechanisms to meet any emerging challenges. He directed the officers to further strengthen area domination, surveillance, route security and facilitation measures for the pilgrims.

title: Schrödinger's Strait of Hormuz: Open or Closed?
description: Despite a ship being struck in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday and another incident over the weekend, some vessels continue to travel the strait on both the Iranian and Omani sides. However, overall traffic remains significantly reduced compared to pre-conflict levels, with daily ship movements down from over 140 in February to approximately 30-40 currently. Bloomberg News Energy & Commodities Reporter Stephen Stapczynski joins Bloomberg This Weekend to explain to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini that safety concerns persist, causing ship owners, captains, and energy exporters to reassess the risks of passage through this critical waterway. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ram Madhav rejects report of India-Pakistan Track 2 talks in Colombo
description:
Srinagar, June 27: Senior BJP leader and India Foundation president Ram Madhav on Saturday rejected a media report claiming he participated in fresh India-Pakistan Track 2 talks in Colombo, calling it a "complete spin to a non-story."
Madhav said the event in question was the International Institute for Strategic Studies' (IISS) annual South Asia Dialogue, attended by participants from several countries and not a bilateral Track 2 engagement.
In a post on X, Madhav wrote: "Totally wrong portrayal. It was not any track 2 dialogue. It was IISS annual South Asia Dialogue which was attended by scholars from India, Sri Lanka, US, UK, Afghanistan n Pakistan. Even officials attended this annual dialogue in d past. No track 2 is held with so many countries. I did not attend d 2-day dialogue. I was invited to speak at one session which I did n left. Complete spin to a non-story."
The clarification came after the Hindustan Times reported that Indian and Pakistani delegations comprising politicians, retired military officials and diplomats held informal discussions on the sidelines of the IISS security conference in Colombo this week.
According to the report, the Indian delegation included Ram Madhav, former Army chief Gen. M.M. Naravane and former diplomat Ruchi Ghanashyam, while the Pakistani side comprised Foreign Ministry Director General (South Asia and SAARC) Sajjad Haider Khan, Pakistan Peoples Party leader Sherry Rehman and retired Maj. Gen. Isfandiyar Ali Khan Pataudi.
The newspaper reported that the Indian and Pakistani delegates held separate discussions over a day and a half at the Hilton Colombo. It said the talks covered issues including terrorism, sharing of waters of cross-border rivers, strengthening communications during crises and possible measures to manage and prevent escalation of tensions.
The report described the Colombo meeting as the latest in a series of back-channel contacts between the two countries since the May 2025 military conflict following the Pahalgam terror attack and India's Operation Sindoor.
Quoting an unnamed Indian official, the Hindustan Times said the government maintained that there was "no official connection" to such contacts and reiterated its position that "terrorism and talks cannot go together." The report also noted that attempts to portray the Colombo discussions as a Track 1.5 dialogue because of the participation of a serving Pakistani foreign ministry official were dismissed, as there were no serving Indian government officials at the meeting.
The newspaper further reported that the discussions did not produce any significant outcomes.

title: Civilian killed in explosion near LoC in Kashmir's Gulmarg
description:
Srinagar, June 27: A civilian was killed in an explosion near the Line of Control (LoC) in the Gulmarg area of north Kashmir's Baramulla district on Saturday, officials said.
Preliminary reports suggest the explosion was caused by an unexploded shell in a forward area close to the LoC. The victim died on the spot, officials said.
As per news agency KNO, an unexploded shell went off near Asha Post in the Sumli Wali Dhok area near Gulmarg on Saturday afternoon.
The deceased has been identified as Zabir Ahmad Bajad, son of Mohammad Rafiq Bajad, a resident of Galibal, Chandoosa in Baramulla district.
Police and other security agencies have launched an investigation to ascertain the exact cause of the explosion.

title: Police crack ATM swap fraud case in Banihal; 3 interstate gang members arrested from Leh
description:
Banihal, June 27: In a major success against financial fraud, Police Station Banihal has solved an ATM swapping case with the arrest of three alleged members of an interstate gang from Leh, Ladakh.
The case came to light on June 17, 2026, when a woman approached Police Station Banihal with a complaint alleging that she was duped at an ATM near Bus Stand Banihal. Acting swiftly, police registered FIR No. 127/2026 under Sections 303(2) and 318(4) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) against unidentified persons and initiated an investigation.
Using technical analysis and human intelligence inputs, the investigating team traced the suspects to Leh, following which a special police team was deputed to Ladakh.
After sustained surveillance, the team successfully apprehended three accused persons identified as Arjun, son of Raj Kumar, resident of Muzaffarnagar, Uttar Pradesh , Shivam Kumar, son of Jagpal Singh, resident of Bijnor, Uttar Pradesh and Monti, son of Pahal Singh, resident of Saharanpur, Uttar Pradesh
After completing legal formalities and obtaining transit remand from the competent court in Leh, the accused were brought back to Police Station Banihal for further investigation.
Police said the investigation is underway to ascertain the involvement of the accused in other similar ATM fraud cases and to recover the embezzled amount.
Banihal Police has advised the public to remain cautious while using ATMs and not to share ATM cards, PINs, or banking details with unknown persons.

title: ACB attaches house, land parcels, gold in DA case against former DC Office Naazir
description:
Srinagar, June 27: The Jammu and Kashmir Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) on Saturday said it has attached multiple movable and immovable properties, including a residential house, land parcels and gold ornaments, belonging to a former Naazir in the Deputy Commissioner’s Office, Srinagar, in connection with a disproportionate assets case.
In a statement issued, an ACB spokesperson said that the attached assets belong to accused namely Imtiyaz Ahmad Bhat S/o Lt. Gh. Mohammad Bhat R/o Firdousabad Batamaloo Srinagar, who was posted as Naazir in the office of Deputy Commissioner Srinagar. The attachments were made in connection with FIR No. 09/2025 registered at Police Station ACB Srinagar.
The attached properties include a single-storey tin roofed residential house along with land measuring (06) marlas & 172 Sqft under survey No.3173/2683/2385/478min Khewat No. 349 Khata No. 999 situated at Firdous Abad Batamaloo Srinagar owned by accused and his spouse; land measuring 10 marals under Khasra No. 1820 min Khewat No.754,Khata No.1215 recorded in the name  of accused`s daughter under mutation No.3525 situated at Nambal Mouza Nowgam Srinagar; land measuring 04 marlas 136 Sqft under survey No.2410 min Khewat No.316, Khata No. 579 recorded in the name of accused`s wife under mutation No.3526 situated at Nambal Mouza Nowgam Srinagar; land measuring 08 marals 136 sqft under survey No. 1821 min, Khewat No. 754, Khata No.1216 recorded in the name of accused`s wife under mutation No.3526 situated at Nambal Mouza Nowgam Srinagar; and gold articles weighing approximately 1266 grams seized during investigation.
The spokesperson said that on 22-05-2025, the instant case was registered by PS Anti Corruption Bureau Srinagar on the outcome of secret verification into the allegation of accumulation of assets disproportionate to known sources of income by one Imtiyaz Ahmad Bhat S/O Lt. Gh. Mohammad Bhat R/O Firdousabad Batamaloo Srinagar, who was posted as Naazir in the office of Deputy Commissioner Srinagar and investigation set into motion.
He said that during the course of investigation, initially the PS ACB Srinagar searched the house of above named alleged accused person and found property in form of copper utensils, golden ornaments and other luxury items lying in his house, which were subsequently seized in the instant case. The accused person was rounded up in the instant case, who is presently on bail on court directions.
Investigations continued and came to fore that the above named public servant has accumulated huge assets, both moveable and immovable, disproportionate to known sources of income. In this connection this Police Station after obtaining approval from competent authority constituted two teams head by Dy.SP level officers for attachment of above mentioned illegal properties. The teams were deputed to Batamaloo and Nowgam Srinagar for carrying out the process of attachment. After fulfilling all legal requirements, the above mentioned properties of the accused were attached on the spot. Sign boards were erected for information of general public.
The agency urged general public not to buy or exchange attached house or land in question.

title: Who is Prabhjeet Singh? Uber veteran chosen to lead OpenAI’s growth in India - DETAILS
description: OpenAI has appointed former Uber India President Prabhjeet Singh as its Managing Director for India. The IIT-IIM alumnus will lead growth, partnerships, enterprise adoption, and AI ecosystem development.
content: Prabhjeet Singh, a seasoned technology executive best known for leading Uber’s operations across India and South Asia, has been appointed as Managing Director for OpenAI India. The appointment marks a significant step in OpenAI’s efforts to strengthen its presence in one of the world’s fastest-growing artificial intelligence markets.Announced on Friday, Singh’s new role will place him at the forefront of OpenAI’s expansion plans in India. As the company’s senior-most executive in the country, he will oversee key areas including consumer growth, enterprise adoption, strategic partnerships, policy engagement, and overall business operations.OpenAI said Singh will play an important role in building relationships across the AI ecosystem and helping businesses, institutions, government agencies, and individual users leverage artificial intelligence more effectively. He will report to Asia Pacific Managing Director Kiran Mani and is expected to formally join the company in September.OpenAI Sharpens Focus on IndiaSingh’s appointment comes as OpenAI accelerates its efforts to expand in India through collaborations with enterprises, developers, and policymakers. The company established its first office in New Delhi in November 2025 and has repeatedly highlighted India as a strategic growth market.OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman has often spoken about India’s growing importance in the global AI landscape. During the AI Summit earlier this year, Altman praised the country’s rapid adoption of technology and its willingness to invest across the artificial intelligence value chain.He described India’s AI momentum as remarkable, noting that the country’s startup and developer ecosystem could witness a significant wave of innovation driven by advancements in artificial intelligence.Before joining OpenAI, Singh spent nearly 11 years at Uber, including six years as President of India and South Asia. In that role, he managed the ride-hailing company’s business across India, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka, helping drive expansion in one of the world’s largest mobility markets.Having joined Uber in 2015, Singh steadily climbed the leadership ladder before taking charge of the India and South Asia region in 2020. During his tenure, Uber expanded its footprint across more than 125 cities, offering services ranging from bike and auto rides to car bookings and metro ticketing.Uber recently reiterated its long-term commitment to India, with global CEO Dara Khosrowshahi announcing plans to establish the company’s first data centre in the country through a partnership with the Adani Group.Following the announcement of Singh’s exit, Uber thanked him for his contributions over the past decade and wished him success in his new leadership role.Academic Background and Early CareerSingh holds degrees from IIT Kharagpur and IIM Ahmedabad, two of India’s most prestigious educational institutions. Before joining Uber, he worked at McKinsey & Company as an Associate Partner, gaining extensive experience in business strategy and operations.In 2022, IIM Ahmedabad recognised his professional achievements with its Young Alumni Achiever’s Award.The leadership change comes at a time when major global AI companies are increasing investments in India. The country’s large pool of developers, rising adoption of AI tools, and growing demand from businesses have made it a key destination for technology firms.Industry observers believe Singh’s track record of scaling technology-driven businesses and navigating complex markets could help OpenAI strengthen its position in India and accelerate adoption of AI solutions across sectors. His appointment is widely seen as a strategic move as OpenAI looks to deepen its footprint in one of the most promising AI markets globally.

title: Big financial changes from July 1: Check THESE 6 important updates on ITR, Aadhaar, passport, credit cards
description: Big financial changes from July 1: ​​For individuals filing ITR-1 and ITR-2 forms for Financial Year 2025-26 (Assessment Year 2026-27), the deadline remains July 31, 2026.
content: Big financial changes from July 1: Taxpayers, Aadhaar holders, passport applicants, and bank customers should prepare for several key financial and regulatory updates effective July 1, 2026. These changes span income tax filing, identity document services, credit card benefits, and consumer protection norms.1. ITR filing deadline for AY 2026-27For individuals filing ITR-1 and ITR-2 forms for Financial Year 2025-26 (Assessment Year 2026-27), the deadline remains July 31, 2026. Missing this deadline may attract penalties, restrict taxpayers from opting for certain tax regimes, and limit the ability to carry forward eligible losses.Also Read | ITR filing: How to file ITR without Form 16 using AIS and Form 26AS? - Check here2. Free Aadhaar email update facilityIn a customer-friendly move, the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) has made updating the registered email address on Aadhaar cards completely free for six months.According to an official UIDAI notification, "It has been decided to waive off the charges (i.e., Rs 75) for availing the service of email address update through the Aadhaar mobile application and make it free of cost for a period of six months with effect from July 1, 2026, to December 31, 2026."This facility will be available via the Aadhaar mobile app until the end of 2026.3. SBI credit card reward points changesSBI Card has revised its reward points programme for select co-branded PhonePe SBI Credit Cards, effective July 1, 2026. The changes, impacting PhonePe SBI Credit Card PURPLE and PhonePe SBI Credit Card SELECT BLACK variants, include new caps on reward point earnings and an expanded list of transactions ineligible for rewards.Cardholders are advised to review the updated terms to avoid surprises in reward accumulation.Read More | Is travel insurance mandatory for a trip? From Schengen to Qatar; here's full list of countries where it's a MUST - Details4. HDFC Bank credit card lounge access revisedHDFC Bank credit card users will now need to meet a minimum spend threshold for complimentary lounge access.From July 1, 2026, cardholders become eligible for three complimentary domestic airport lounge visits per calendar quarter only if they spend at least Rs 60,000 in the preceding quarter. For example, to enjoy lounge access in the July–September 2026 quarter, spending of Rs 60,000 or more between April and June 2026 is required.5. Increase in passport feesPassport applicants will have to pay higher fees starting July 1, 2026. The Ministry of External Affairs has hiked service fees for both normal and Tatkaal passports (for applications in India and overseas). Exact revised amounts have not been detailed in the initial announcement, but applicants are expected to incur additional costs.6. RBI tightens norms against Mis-sellingIn a major boost for consumer protection, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has introduced stricter rules to prevent mis-selling of financial products by banks. Effective July 1, 2026, customers who are mis-sold products will be entitled to a full refund along with compensation for any losses incurred.This framework aims to enhance accountability and transparency in the sale of banking and third-party financial products.

title: Lucknow Leopards Championship: The Tournament That Could Change Indian Picklebal...
description: The Lucknow Leopards Championship is set to become a defining stop on the Road to IPBL, where India's top players will compete not only for the country's richest prize purse but also for a shot at professional pickleball.
content: In India's rapidly growing pickleball landscape, the Indian Pickleball League emerged as a symbol of professionalism and a testament to the growing confidence of investors betting big on the sport's unprecedented global rise. The IPBL, India's official league for the sport sanctioned by the Indian Pickleball Association, is now gearing up for its second edition after a successful inaugural season in December 2025 in New Delhi, where Mumbai Smashers were crowned champions following a roller-coaster tournament packed with thrilling contests.The IPBL saw Indian players like Harsh Mehta, Arjun Singh, Pearl Amalsadiwala, Shreya Chakraborty and Naomi Amalsadiwala rub shoulders with foreign stars, elevating their game to an unprecedented level that even surprised them. It gave them an opportunity to realise their true potential under the spotlight, with fans following every move on the court and opponents closely studying their styles. Scrutiny became part of the experience.Strategy was key. On several occasions, the Indian players even got the better of their overseas counterparts. In many ways, the IPBL emerged as a launching pad for Indian talent, with its impact becoming increasingly evident. Arjun Singh went on to dominate the US Open Pickleball Championship, winning three gold medals, while Aditya Ruhela secured a PPA Tour deal and Harsh Mehta joined the APP Tour. Most recently, Arjun and Naomi reached the BIDV Cup final after stunning victories over Quang Duong and Shelby Bates, followed by Richard Livornese Jr. and Megan Fudge in the semifinals. First tracking the IPBL auction Preparations for a bigger and grander second season of the IPBL are currently underway. In this direction, Lucknow Leopards, one of the flagship franchises of the IPBL, announced the Lucknow Leopards Championship on June 26 at a pickleball event in Gurgaon, reaffirming their unwavering commitment to the growth of pickleball in India. The tournament will be the country's richest in pickleball, carrying a total prize purse of Rs 10 lakh, with each of the five pro categories offering Rs 2 lakh.For the upcoming season, the IPA has rolled out Road to IPBL, a pathway programme through which standout performers will earn the opportunity to enter the IPBL Player Auction, putting them in contention to represent a franchise in the Indian Pickleball League.The Lucknow Leopards Championship will be part of that pathway. Designated as a PWR 700 event, it will offer players crucial national ranking points - among the highest available at any domestic pickleball tournament in India. As a result, top players from across the country are expected to compete.Scheduled for August 22 and 23 at Bennett University, the Lucknow Leopards Championship offers players a golden opportunity to boost their chances of securing an IPBL contract. A title-winning performance in the Pro Men's Singles, Pro Men's Doubles, Pro Women's Singles, Pro Women's Doubles, or Pro Mixed Doubles categories is expected to fast-track players into the talent scouting process for the league's six franchises, including Lucknow Leopards, who will be aiming for an improved campaign after finishing third in the inaugural season.Shaleen Jain, the representative of Lucknow Leopards, is delighted with the launch of the tournament."I feel this is a great opportunity because we are calling it the Road to IPBL. Now IPBL is one of the most prestigious tournaments in the country," he told Pickleball Now.The significance of the Lucknow Leopards Championship lies in its ability to provide players with a platform to shine, attract the attention of franchise owners, and earn professional contracts. With the country's top-rated players set to compete, the championship is poised to become one of India's most anticipated pickleball tournaments.Shaleen acknowledged this dynamic. "So if you're good enough and if the IPBL notices you, they can fast track you into the auction list even though you were not the last year. So I think it's a good opportunity. But the other thing is that you will be playing with the best of the best. So all the top players of the country are going to participate in this tournament, widely anticipated tournament. So you get to know how good you are or how do you compare with the best people," he added.'Premium tournament combo'Aalap Sharma, the Treasurer of the Indian Pickleball Association, said, "The Lucknow Leopards Championship is not just another tournament, it is a premium career accelerator in Indian pickleball. I would term it as the most premium tournament combo."Aalap also pointed to the tournament's monetary appeal, which he said would set a new benchmark in Indian pickleball."IPA-PWR 700 points: This elite ranking tier means winners can leapfrog into national contention, rewriting their career trajectory overnight. Fixed Rs 2 lakh per category: Unlike variable prize pools in other major tournaments, this guaranteed payout across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles sets a new benchmark for financial rewards in Indian pickleball," he said.With the Lucknow Leopards Championship set to become a marquee stop on the Road to IPBL, Aalap said exceptional performances at the tournament could open the door to professional opportunities for players."Performances here double as auditions for league franchises and selectors are watching closely. Impressing at this event could mean contracts, sponsorships, and entry into India’s first professional pickleball ecosystem," Aalap signed off.

title: Ram Temple Trust rocked over donation row
Prince William becomes richer than King Charles: Inside his $1.6 billion Duchy of Cornwall empire and Kensington Palace's 73% female workforce
description: Prince William has become a billionaire with a $1.6 billion fortune, surpassing King Charles in personal wealth through the historic Duchy of Cornwall, while unveiling plans to reinvest part of the estate into affordable housing, environmental projects and local communities.
content: Prince William, the Prince of Wales and heir to the British throne, has officially entered the billionaire's club, with newly published accounts revealing a net worth of $1.6 billion, surpassing his father King Charles in personal wealth for the first time. The milestone, disclosed through the annual Sovereign Grant accounts, places the 44-year-old future king ahead of the reigning monarch, who holds a personal fortune of $846 million.The Estate That Built a BillionAt the heart of William's wealth lies the Duchy of Cornwall, a royal estate established in 1337 by King Edward III to financially support the heir to the British throne.Today, the estate spans more than:205 square miles51,800 hectares19 counties across EnglandExtensive holdings across Cornwall, Devon, the Isles of Scilly, Bath and LondonIts portfolio includes:Agricultural landCoastal estatesCommercial propertyResidential developmentsHistoric buildingsUrban real estateEnvironmental land holdingsThe Duchy remains one of Britain's largest privately owned property portfolios.  The Duchy is no ordinary real estate portfolio. Its holdings encompass rural farmland, coastal properties, urban estates and some of the most historically significant land in England, covering territories from Cornwall to the Isles of Scilly, through the Bath countryside, the Dartmoor landscape and into the heart of Kennington in London. It is an empire built not over years but over nearly seven centuries of careful stewardship and royal inheritance.The Numbers: Income, Taxes and TransparencyIn 2025-26, William received a private income of $28.5 million from the Duchy's operations. Since he became Prince of Wales four years ago, he has paid more than $26 million in taxes, in what marks the first time the royal household has disclosed such detailed personal tax information in a bid for greater transparency around how the monarchy is funded.During the 2025-26 financial year, William received:$28.5 million in private income from the DuchyPaid $7.76 million in income and capital gains taxHas contributed more than $26 million in taxes since becoming Prince of WalesThe royal household disclosed these figures publicly as part of its ongoing transparency initiative.In the most recent tax year for which figures are available, William paid $7.76 million in income and capital gains tax, following $8.34 million the previous year. The disclosures represent a notable departure from historical royal practice, with both William and King Charles choosing to make their private tax contributions public as part of what appears to be a deliberate effort to demonstrate accountability to British taxpayers who fund the Sovereign Grant.  William Richer Than the KingThe revelation that William now outstrips his father in personal wealth is a striking inversion of the traditional assumption that the reigning monarch occupies the pinnacle of royal financial power. King Charles, 77, controlled the Duchy of Cornwall for 53 years before passing it on to William, building his own fortune across more than five decades as its beneficiary. Yet Charles's current net worth of $846 million, while substantial, falls well short of the $1.6 billion that William has accumulated through the same estate in just four years of stewardship.Although King Charles managed the Duchy of Cornwall for more than five decades before becoming monarch, William's inherited estate is now valued at approximately $1.6 billion, almost double Charles' estimated personal wealth.For comparison:Prince William: $1.6 billionKing Charles III: $846 millionQueen Elizabeth II (at her death): $447 millionThe appreciation of the Duchy's land values over recent decades has significantly boosted William's overall net worth.  By way of further historical context, the late Queen Elizabeth was reportedly worth $447 million at the time of her death, making William's fortune more than three times that of his grandmother at the peak of her reign, a reflection of how significantly the Duchy's assets have appreciated in value over recent decades.Charles's fortune is itself not static. The Sunday Times Rich List recently valued his personal wealth at $846 million, representing a rise of approximately $40 million compared to the previous year, driven in part by his income from the Duchy of Lancaster, which serves as the sovereign's private estate in the same way the Duchy of Cornwall provides for the heir.  The Dartmoor Prison GestureIn one of the more striking financial decisions to emerge alongside the wealth disclosure, William has chosen to forgo the income generated by Dartmoor Prison, a Category C facility in Devon that has been standing empty since July 2024 after elevated concentrations of radon, a naturally occurring harmful gas, were detected in the accommodation used by inmates. The prison earns the Duchy of Cornwall approximately $1.9 million annually under a 25-year lease signed with the Ministry of Justice in 2022, a contract that was put in place before William became Prince of Wales.Rather than accepting this income while the prison remains closed and the surrounding community in Princetown faces economic uncertainty, William has asked for the money to be redirected from 2026-27 onward to support the regeneration of the local community. A community-driven renewal fund will be established to deliver social, economic and environmental benefits to Princetown and its residents.William's private secretary Ian Patrick articulated the thinking behind the decision, saying the Prince knows that for many people in Princetown, the prison has long been part of the fabric of the community, and that its closure has created genuine uncertainty, not just about jobs and businesses, but about the future of the town itself. He added that William felt strongly the income should remain in the community while those questions remain unanswered.Selling the Crown Jewels of LandIn what represents perhaps the most significant strategic decision William has announced regarding the Duchy, he has revealed plans to sell 20 percent of the Cornwall estate over the next ten years, raising a projected $670 million that he intends to invest in affordable housing and environmental projects across Cornwall, Dartmoor, the Isles of Scilly, the Bath area and Kennington.In one of the biggest strategic changes in the Duchy's history, William has announced plans to sell 20 percent of the Cornwall estate over the next decade.The move is expected to raise approximately $670 million, with proceeds earmarked for:Affordable housingEnvironmental restorationSustainable developmentCommunity projects across Cornwall, Dartmoor, Bath, the Isles of Scilly and LondonThe decision represents one of the most significant reforms ever proposed for the centuries-old estate.The announcement represents a philosophical departure from centuries of royal land accumulation and retention, with the future king choosing to liquidate a portion of one of Britain's most historically preserved private estates in order to address pressing contemporary needs around housing and environmental restoration. In a statement, William said there is so much good they can do, adding that he is trying to prioritise actions that will make a material difference to people living in those areas.  The latest royal accounts also reveal that Kensington Palace now employs:74 staff members73% female workforce14.9% ethnic minority representationThe increase reflects William and Catherine's growing public and charitable responsibilities.Prince William's Biggest Assets at a GlanceEstimated Net Worth: $1.6 billionMain Wealth Source: Duchy of CornwallEstate Size: 205 square milesLand Holdings: 51,800 hectaresAnnual Duchy Income: $28.5 millionTaxes Paid Since 2022: Over $26 millionMajor Future Investment: $670 million from planned land salesWhat William's Billionaire Status MeansPrince William's billionaire status reflects far more than inherited wealth. By publishing personal tax figures, redirecting controversial income toward community projects and pledging to invest hundreds of millions into affordable housing and environmental initiatives, the future king is attempting to redefine how Britain's royal wealth is managed in the modern era.As the heir to the throne, William is no longer simply the future monarch—he has become one of Britain's most influential landowners and wealth stewards, with decisions that could shape both the monarchy's public image and the communities connected to its historic estates for decades to come.

title: TET पेपरफुटीमध्ये पोलिसांचा धक्कादायक खुलासा; तब्बल ‘इतक्या’ कोटी रुपयांत पेपरची ठरली किंमत, पण त्या आधीच फुटलं बिंग
description: मुंबई | Mumbaiनीट पेपरफुटीनंतर महाराष्ट्रात खळबळ उडवून देणाऱ्या टीईटी पेपर फुटीचेही आता दिल्ली अन् हरयाणा कनेक्शन समोर आले आहे. उद्या २८ जून रोजी होणाऱ्या टीईटी परीक्षेचा पेपर फुटल्याने ही परीक्षा रद्द करण्यात आली असून पोलिसांनी तीन आरोपींना अटक केली आहे. सहारा विमानतळ परिसरातून या संशयितांना अटक केली असून त्यांच्यावर गुन्हा दाखल करत बेड्या ठोकल्या आहे. […]
content:
मुंबई | Mumbaiनीट पेपरफुटीनंतर महाराष्ट्रात खळबळ उडवून देणाऱ्या टीईटी पेपर फुटीचेही आता दिल्ली अन् हरयाणा कनेक्शन समोर आले आहे. उद्या २८ जून रोजी होणाऱ्या टीईटी परीक्षेचा पेपर फुटल्याने ही परीक्षा रद्द करण्यात आली असून पोलिसांनी तीन आरोपींना अटक केली आहे. सहारा विमानतळ परिसरातून या संशयितांना अटक केली असून त्यांच्यावर गुन्हा दाखल करत बेड्या ठोकल्या आहे. त्यानंतर, अपर पोलीस आयुक्त अशोक दुधे यांनी पत्रकार परिषद घेऊन टीईटी पेपर फुटीसंदर्भात माहिती दिली.



एका पेपरची किंमत दीड कोटी रुपयेटीईटी पेपरफुटीच्या संदर्भाने भिवंडी पोलिसांनी पत्रकार परिषद घेऊन काही धक्कादायक खुलासे केले आहेत. भिवंडी पोलिसांनी तीन आरोपींना अटक केली असून यातील २ आरोपी बिहारमधील आहेत, तर एक आरोपी हरियाणामधला आहे. एका पेपरची किंमत तब्बल दीड कोटी रुपये ठरली होती, असे भिवंडीचे अतिरिक्त पोलीस आयुक्त अशोक दुधे यांनी सांगितले. तसेच पेपरफुटीचा तपास करण्यासाठी विशेष पोलीस पथकाची स्थापना केली असून एसीपींच्या नेतृत्वाखाली ८ पोलीस निरीक्षक आणि इतर कर्मचारी काम करून पेपरफुटीचा तपास करतील, अशीही माहिती अतिरिक्त पोलीस आयुक्त दुधे यांनी दिली.



TET Exam Cancelled: पेपर कसा फुटला ते पुन्हा पेपर कधी होणार?महाराष्ट्र परिक्षा परिषदेची सविस्तर माहिती



मुख्य संशयित अद्याप फरारटीईटी पेपर विक्रीसाठी आरोपी दिल्लीतून आले होते. त्यामध्ये, राजीव शाह (बिहार) (४५), आकाश कुमार (बिहार)(३०) आणि धीरज कुमार (हरियाणा)(२८) अशी आरोपींची नावे आहे. मात्र, याप्रकरणातील मुख्य आरोपी अद्यापही फरार आहे. पोलिसांच्या झडतीत ४ पेपरचे सेट मिळाले असून मोबाईल, डेबिट कार्ड देखील मिळाले आहेत. पोलिसांनी, भारतीय न्याय संहिता कलम ३१८(४) ३१६(५) ६१(२), सह महाराष्ट्र परीक्षाअधिनियम २०२४ या प्रमाणे गुन्हा दाखल केला आहे.



पोलिसांना मिळालेल्या गोपनीय माहितीच्या आधारे आरोपींसाठी ट्रॅप लावण्यात आला होता. आरोपी दिल्लीवरुन पेपर विकण्यासाठी भिंवडीला आले होते, कोणासाठी पेपर विकायला आले होते, कोणाला विकला, कुणी घेतला? या सर्व बाबींचा तपास करण्यात येत आहे. विशेष म्हणजे, आरोपींकडून जे पेपर विकत घेणार होते, ते पुढे जाऊन पैसे घेऊन पुढे विकणार होते, अशी माहिती समोर आल्याचेही पोलीस आयुक्त अशोक दुधे यांनी दिली. उमेदवार- कोचिंग सेंटर, तसेच दुसऱ्या कुणी व्यक्ती पेपर विकत घेणार होते का? हे देखील तपासत आहोत, असे पोलीस अधिकारी दुधे यांनी सांगितले.

title: Apple and Audi alumni have made a luxe EV based on the moon buggy
description: The Amble One is a street-legal $25,000 electric buggy designed for luxury resorts.
content:
It seems to be the week for cheap EVs. Right after the production model of the Slate electric truck was revealed, complete with a bump in range, a new European entrant in electric mobility is launching out of stealth mode today and plans to bring its own affordable yet stylish rides to market.
Amble's founders worked at Audi and Ford, started Cowboy ebikes, and cofounded Forpeople, the creative agency that works for, among others, Nio EVs, Arc’teryx, and Herman Miller. Indeed, Amble's design lead, Julian Hoenig, worked on the infamously canceled Apple car, which goes some way to explaining how this, the $25,000 Amble One, looks like it could have driven straight out of Cupertino, despite hailing from Lisbon, Portugal.
The Amble One is a street-legal, stripped-down electric buggy designed for the kind of places where a normal car feels out of place. Coastal paths, private estates, and those dusty tracks between luxury hotel villas and the sea. Think of it as if Apple decided it was going to design a golf cart, then took the project even further.Read full article

title: My 85-year-old Jeep is perfect for driving to the shops
description:
Ready for action: Simple and reliable Jeep is easy to work on and appreciate

"We always get the first car parking space at Asda!" jokes Ian Watson about his Willys Jeep, which he has owned for 10 years.
"With the machine gun mounts - I have a couple of 30-calibre guns at home that fit them - it looks pretty serious." I hope he removes the - fake? - hand grenade attached to the dashboard when he and wife Viv pop into the supermarket.
Ian's Jeep is an early example - a 1941 'Slat Grille' (when Ford also began producing jeeps for the war effort, it swapped the grille for a simpler piece of pressed steel to speed up production).

"It was shipped out to the South Pacific just two days after Pearl Harbour," says Ian. "After the war, the Americans abandoned much of their equipment, and this Jeep remained in the Philippines. Eventually, it was run into the ground, but a museum in Manila, recognising its value as an early model, bought and restored it. When the museum closed, all the exhibits were sold off on eBay, which is how I came to buy it. It was more or less in its current condition and cost me £17,000, but today an early 'slat grille' Jeep goes for around £34,000, so I haven't done badly."
Ian's Jeep is powered by the Willys 'Go Devil' engine that most Jeeps used. It's a 2.2-litre inline four-cylinder flathead, or sidevalve, motor. "It leaks oil like a sieve, and because it was used in a hot country, it has a second radiator to keep it cool," he says.

"It makes only around 60hp but just a little more than 100lb ft at 2000rpm, so it pulls well from idle. In fact, it feels a bit like a tractor! It's certainly as slow as one, with 45mph the fastest I've had out of it. Not that I'd want to go much faster. The brakes are hydraulic but aren't very good. It has cart springs at the back, too, so you do get thrown about. You have to hang on; I'm conscious I might fall out, so we've fitted door straps. Soldiers back in the war were a lot smaller and could cling on more easily than a big fella like me."
Ian's Jeep has a three-speed manual gearbox and, being permanent four-wheel drive, a transfer box with high and low ranges. The handbrake works on the driveshaft and, says Ian, holds the vehicle securely. He adds: "A Jeep is a reasonably simple thing. I can do most jobs on it, but those I can't I farm out to a Jeep specialist in Milford, near Godalming. Spare parts are easy to come by. Because so many Jeeps were made, it's possible to get almost any bit that you need. It might be original too, although there are many new parts now coming out of India."

Ian and Viv do around 500 miles a year in their Jeep. "Because it's quite slow, it can be frustrating for those drivers following us, but people do love to see it about. When we first bought it, a lot of veterans would come up and reminisce, but they've passed on now. I keep it in my garage, one half of which has military memorabilia associated with it. The other side, where I park my 1968 Mini Moke, has 1960s memorabilia."

Trevor Nelson to take break from BBC Radio 2 for medical reasons: “I’m concentrating on getting better”
description:
The legendary DJ said he is focussing on getting "back behind the mic and the decks"
The post Trevor Nelson to take break from BBC Radio 2 for medical reasons: “I’m concentrating on getting better” appeared first on NME.
content:
BBC DJ and presenter Trevor Nelson has announced he is taking a break from his BBC Radio 2 show for medical reasons.
The Radio 2 and 1Xtra presenter, who has been off air this week after undergoing tests, announced that he is “taking a little break from my work commitments” due to “health issues” yesterday (June 26).
He did not go into detail about his diagnosis, but told his Instagram followers that he was now “concentrating on getting better”.
“Some of you may already have noticed that I’ve not been on my daily Radio 2 slot this week,” he told fans. “After a routine check-up I was advised to have some follow up tests. As a result, I will be taking some further time off. As I’m sure you can appreciate with health issues, it’s important to deal with facts and not speculate.
“So I’m concentrating on getting better, being back to 100% me and to getting back behind the mic and the decks.”
Earlier this week, Nelson received a special prize at the Television and Radio Industries Club (Tric) Awards, recognising his broadcasting career across radio and television. This year, he also won the Judge’s Special Recognition Award at the ARIAs, the industry’s yearly awards, and celebrated 30 years at the BBC.

  










View this post on Instagram

























Nelson presents weekdays between 2 to 4pm on Radio 2, and on BBC Radio 1Xtra each Sunday, at 11am until 1pm.
The BBC’s director of music, Lorna Clarke said: “Everyone at BBC Radio 2 and BBC Radio 1Xtra wishes Trevor a very speedy recovery and we all look forward to him returning to his shows whenever he is ready.”
Messages of love and support from artists including Stormzy, Spice Girl’s Mel B, Beverley Knight, Jools Holland and Alesha Dixon have also been flooding in under Nelson’s post.
“Health. First and always,” noted singer Knight, while Corinne Bailey Rae added: “Well done for listening to your body.”
Laverne of 6 Music and Radio 4 said: “Sending lots of love and keeping everything crossed for you. I know how that can feel – take it one step at a time and try not to jump ahead.”
Vernon Kay paid tribute on his show on Friday, saying: “Everyone at BBC Radio 2, including the mid-morning show are sending Trevor all the very very best wishes, we absolutely love him to bits.
“He’s a stalwart broadcaster within the UK – I played golf with him a couple of weeks ago and he was in a buoyant mood. And I know that this challenge is something that Trevor is gonna face head on.
“So Trevor we are sending you all our love and hopefully see you on the links very soon my friend.”
Next week, Clara Amfo will cover his weekday show, after which, DJ Spoony will take over from July 6 until further notice.
Listeners can hear him present The Music is Black concert from Hackney Empire on Sunday night (June 28) on BBC Radio 2, which was pre-recorded last month.
The post Trevor Nelson to take break from BBC Radio 2 for medical reasons: “I’m concentrating on getting better” appeared first on NME.

title: ‘The Bear’ honours late guest star Rob Reiner with heartfelt nod in series finale
description:
The beloved filmmaker appeared as a restaurant consultant in three episodes of the fourth season
The post ‘The Bear’ honours late guest star Rob Reiner with heartfelt nod in series finale appeared first on NME.
content:
The Bear paid tribute to the late legendary director and actor Rob Reiner in its final episode.

READ MORE: ‘The Bear’ season five review: tuck into the final helping of this heartfelt kitchen drama

Created by Christopher Storer, the acclaimed FX kitchen drama stars Jeremy Allen White as Carmen “Carmy” Berzatto, Ayo Edebiri as Sydney Adamu, Ebon Moss-Bachrach as Richard “Richie” Jerimovich and Abby Elliott as Natalie “Sugar” Berzatto.
Before Reiner’s tragic death, he appeared in a three-episode arc in The Bear’s fourth season as restaurant consultant Albert Schnurr. The 78-year-old filmmaker and his wife, Michele Singer Reiner, 70, were found murdered in their home in the Brentwood area of Los Angeles late last year.
In the last episode of the new and final season, line cook Ebra (Edwin Lee Gibson) calls Schnurr to deliver news about the future of the restaurant, saying:  “Albert, we are in business. It’s perfect. I will have all the documents, email it to you immediately. Anything else I can do?”
While viewers don’t hear what’s said by Albert, Ebra pauses and then says: “As you wish,” a nod to Cary Elwes’ Westley in the Reiner-directed 1987 classic The Princess Bride.

The Bear Season 5 is supposed to begin filming today in Chicago, not sure how they will go on without Rob Reiner  pic.twitter.com/UX01wLTY94
— chefkids (@girlflopping) December 15, 2025

Reiner’s son Nick, 32, was arrested in connection with their deaths last year and was later charged with two counts of first-degree murder with “special circumstances”, pleading not guilty to the charges at a court appearance in February.




Tributes poured in for Reiner after the news broke in December, with the likes of Jack Black, Jane Fonda, Stephen King, Barack and Michelle Obama, Paul McCartney, and Meg Ryan all penning moving odes to the late filmmaker.
As for The Bear, its final season premiered last night (June 26), and picks up after Carmy’s decision to step away from the restaurant industry, leaving Sydney, Richie and Natalie to carry The Bear forward without him at the centre of the kitchen.
With money running out, a possible sale looming, and a torrential storm hitting Chicago, the team are forced to pull together for one last service as they try to keep the restaurant alive and chase the recognition they have been working towards.
In a four-star review of season five, NME wrote: “The exact fate of The Bear will be discovered by viewers when the final episode arrives, but the fifth and final season is a suitable send-off to these unlikely comrades. It might not reach the boiling temperatures of the show at its peak, but it’s a satisfying final course.”




The post ‘The Bear’ honours late guest star Rob Reiner with heartfelt nod in series finale appeared first on NME.

title: Missing Glastonbury? There’s a festival on your doorstep that’s saving grassroots venues
description:
The Everywhere At Once festival brings huge names and rising talent to hundreds of venues across the UK this weekend. Artists and venue bosses tell us why you should get involved, and how without these gig spaces we wouldn't have Glasto acts or Oasis
The post Missing Glastonbury? There’s a festival on your doorstep that’s saving grassroots venues appeared first on NME.
content:
This weekend sees the launch of the inaugural Everywhere At Once festival – with huge names and rising stars taking to hundreds of stages in grassroots venues across the UK in a bid to entertain the masses while we sit out Glastonbury. Rizzle Kicks and Inspiral Carpets are among the artists taking part, explaining to NME why this event matters.

READ MORE: The ticket levy that could save grassroots venues and artists: what happens next?

Hundreds of thousands would have flocked to Worthy Farm this weekend, however with the festival taking its traditional fallow year to allow the land to recover, an opportunity arose for the Music Venue Trust and the many essential gig spaces they represent.
“Before we realised it was a Glastonbury fallow year, there was a discussion within the team, Save Our Scene and promoters where we realised this was a turnaround year for the sector,” MVT CEO Mark Davyd told NME. “Money will start to come in from the grassroots levy, things will start to change for the better.”
He continued: “Then there was a quick piece of maths that said if you open up all of the grassroots music venues in the UK at the same time, the capacity of those clubs if full would be 252,000. That’s exactly the size of Glastonbury.”
With the National Lottery also celebrating having reached the milestone of investing over £1billion into music, all the bodies came together and called upon artists including Becky Hill, Fatboy Slim and Tinie Tempah to pull off “a maniac’s dream” of a festival, as Davyd put it, to send out the message: “These venues are still here and still putting on great shows’.”
Harley Sylvester and Jordan Stephens of Rizzle Kicks perform at The Great Escape Festival 2025 in Brighton (Photo by Joseph Okpako/WireImage)
This time last year, Rizzle Kicks were proving themselves to be a surprise highlight of Glastonbury 2025. “It was fucking insane,” recalled Harley Alexander-Sule. “I don’t know if they told people we were giving out free money at 2pm on The Other Stage but it felt like everyone in the festival came down.”
It marked a pivotal moment for the British rap duo’s return, having first split in 2016 before making a celebrated comeback in 2024.
“After we took our hiatus, a lot of shit stuff happened,” Alexander-Sule told NME. “I’m not saying that us leaving made the world shit, but Brexit happened, Trump happened, COVID happened, the cost of living went up in a big way. Everyone’s just been a bit beaten up by the world, then we came back – carefree and not giving a shit. I think people latched on to that.”
Not only that, but over the last decade the UK has lost over 300 of its treasured grassroots music venues – the places where Rizzle Kicks learned the ropes to prepare them for playing to massive crowds at Glasto.
“Back in my day, you’d proper cut your teeth in all those venues,” said Alexander-Sule. “We were between Brighton and London, so before we got signed we’d played 20 venues in Brighton. Brighton’s small, so I’d be very interested to know how many of those still exist. The one we’re playing next – Patterns – we played there a lot. It used to be called Audio and it was one of the first shows we ever did.
“That was the venue I went to see loads of my friends play at. It wasn’t a big venue, but for us as young, up and coming artists, it was a mini bucket list thing to play there or Green Door Store. If you did that at grassroots level it was like, ‘I’ve done it, I’ve completed Brighton!’”
Rizzle Kicks will be returning to Patterns for the Everywhere At Once festival, having not played there in 15 years but excited that “it’s so cool that it’s still knocking about”. So much of the band’s history is embedded in Brighton, that they’re predicting it’ll be “more than a show,” adding: “you can feel the love there because we’re all so in it together.”
Harley Alexander-Sule of Rizzle Kicks performs at Shakedown Festival 2013 in Brighton (Photo by Ollie Millington/Redferns via Getty Images)
Beyond getting back to where it all began, the duo said they needed to get involved as “any light that can be shone on grassroots venues is incredible” – especially now that opportunities are disappearing for new generations of artists.
“There’s nothing wrong with blowing up on TikTok, but I’d be curious to see how these artists go on to cope with performing to audiences that aren’t their own crowds,” said Alexander-Sule. “That’s how you build confidence as an artist: in doing these grassroots venues. My generation of artists may have been the last to make a name for themselves based on their live performance.
“I remember in the 2000s, people would say, ‘Have you heard of these guys? I saw them here’. It was organic and people were seeing music at venues and watching support acts. A&R scouts would be at those venues every night. Now they’re called A& analysts and they just sit on TikTok every night looking at numbers. You don’t get the same authenticity. You can mask a lot on social media and present yourself in a certain way. When you’re going to these live shows, there’s nowhere to hide and people are seeing your authentic self all the time, and you learn things all the time.”
The rapper added that this shouldn’t be chalked up to nostalgia or sentimentality, but simple practicality for future artists to survive.
“We see most of our money come from [playing] live, and I don’t think that would be the case if we didn’t have the opportunity to really hone our craft,” Alexander-Sule added. “Playing live is the biggest thing for us, but if we didn’t put all that time and attention in, we wouldn’t be here.”
Kev Clark, Graham Lambert, Oscar Boon, Stephen Holt and Clint Boon of Inspiral Carpets at The Barrowland Ballroom, 2024 (Photo by Roberto Ricciuti/Redferns)
Another band who cut their teeth on the smaller stages before going on to make history are Madchester legends Inspiral Carpets. They’ll be playing three shows in Chester, Shrewsbury and Stoke across the weekend for Everywhere At Once, simply because “we’re big fans of keeping the live music scene alive,” said keyboarist Clint Boon, “and that includes grassroots venues.”
“Any band of our generation, whether it’s the other Manchester bands, or the bands that followed likes Arctic Monkeys, Fontaines D.C., Wunderhorse – we need those small venues that only hold 150-300 people. It’s impossible otherwise to go straight from being a garage band to then headlining Manchester Apollo. The first step is getting into those smaller local venues like The Boardwalk, The Roadhouse, The Night & Day. They’re an essential part of the journey of any successful band. It’s crucial we keep these venues as part of our culture.”
The plot thickens, as not only did Boon figure out how to become a rockstar in grassroots venues, but in the late ’80s the band famously also hired a then unknown lad by the name of Noel Gallagher as a roadie and technician to tour the circuit with them in his pre-Oasis days.
“I don’t think we’d even made a record when Noel joined us,” Boon recalled. “We might have made our first single when our first singer left and Noel auditioned to replace him. He didn’t get the job, but we took him on as a roadie. We were very much in that world of travelling up and down the UK in a transit van with band members sat in the back on the amplifiers. Noel saw first hand what bands need to do to get on that ladder to success. 
“Still to this day, he gives the Inspirals a lot of credit for taking him on that journey and showing him a lot about the industry and how to operate.”
Oasis live at The Water Rats in London in their early days, 1994. Credit: Ian Dickson/Redferns
Even then, Oasis still still put the the hours in by touring up and down the grassroots circuit in the back of a van.
“Any band credited with organised success has a much longer backstory behind it,” Boon told NME. “For Noel’s first year in Oasis, he was still setting our equipment up and learning. They had a good three or four years before their first big taste of success.”
From Oasis’ first UK headline tour in 1994, only six of the 15 remain open today. Boon admitted that without these spaces of this size, we simply wouldn’t have Inspiral Carpets or Oasis, noting that “it would be impossible to get to where bands like us are without these venues.” 
Looking ahead to the weekend, Boon shared that he had spent a lot time in Stoke Sugarmill as a DJ and as a musician, and still gets excited at the idea of going back into these places. “It’s what we’re all about,” he said. “It doesn’t matter if it’s a small stage, a big stage, an arena or a stadium – it’s what we do. We get up and we play. I’ll never tire of that.”
With a new album ready to go and coming in January, Boon said that Inspiral Carpets would be airing some fresh material at the festival, but ultimately fans should expect “the hits and the crowd favourites.” 
“As a band, we’re performing better than ever. Something magical has happened since we reformed back in 2023,” he added. “It feels like everything has gone up a notch. That joy we’ve got now is quite an infectious thing for people to see. Our happiness and appreciation for where we’re at is what resonates with people, but we can never forget where we came from.” 

  










View this post on Instagram

























Voodoo Daddy’s Showroom in Norwich will be hosting gigs across the weekend, including one of three intimate shows from Tinie Tempah. Live events manager Ben Street told NME how it would “add some extra focus and audience excitement” on a weekend that would otherwise have been lost to Glasto. 
The venue used to be a strip club before becoming a venue under another name and then a hub for the local music community before attracting touring acts. Before long, they were putting on shows like Wunderhorse supporting Sorry and early gigs by the likes of Keo, CMAT and Olivia Dean. 
“This is a brilliant time to do this festival, because there’s been such a focus on the rate at which venues are disappearing and the government not making it that easy for us,” said Street. “There are lot of people that would choose to go to their local venue over a big arena show. That really pushes that hope. We have a really devout community that would rather come to Voodoos to see something new or local than see Oasis with 30,000 other people. These venues are still the beating heart of a lot of communities.”
Street said that the network of venues in UK had “reached catastrophe point,” but were optimistic that “now things are happening.” 
Looking ahead to the weekend, Street had a simple message for music fans: “Come to Voodoo Daddys, but there will be amazing gigs happening at spots all across the country. If you’re looking for something to fill that Glastonbury gap but you want to speak to the band after, this is the chance to see bands up close that wouldn’t normally be playing venues to about 200-300 people. 
“This is the opportunity to see something special. We’re a dive bar and a pizza joint, so we’re the archetype of a venue like this.” 
Everywhere At Once comes at a time when the UK is reeling from “the complete collapse of touring” with only 12 locations left on the primary or secondary circuit for artists where it used to be 28 back in 1994. After decades of decline and the spiralling cost of living and touring, thousands of fans and communities are now without nearby access to live music and countless opportunities for artists are lost. It was recently revealed that over half of UK grassroots venues made no profit in 2025, with 6,000 jobs gone.
MVT CEO Mark Davyd said he hoped this weekend would be an opportunity for fans to rediscover their local venues, and that he was grateful for artists from Tom A. Smith to The Divine Comedy for getting involved.
“We put a call out thinking, ‘Who could we get to go back to a venue we and they really believe in?’ We were trying to shake things up a bit,” he said. “A lot of people will know the story of The Lathums, The Cause, The K’s, but beyond that Becky Hill has a really close association with The Marr’s Bar in Worcester, we wanted to tell the story of where Tinie Tempha has played, and it got very deep and a lot of big things popped up.”
“The scale and scope of it is quite difficult to get your head around. These venues really matter to artists and people have been working really hard to pull off something really special.”
He also said that away from the intimate underplays of bigger artists, fans should also “get out and see something new and give a new artist a chance.”

  










View this post on Instagram

























“Huge input has gone into over 400 venues creating over 1,000 events with around 2,500 artists,” Davyd added. “What it now needs is for the grassroots audience to go out. This is all for them and about celebrating the spaces that are still here after the last 12 years of struggle. Our big message is go to the website, put your postcode in and you can see everything going on around you. Just go celebrate how fantastic it is that these venues are out there. It’s been really hard, but there’s still brilliant stuff happening.
“There’ just about every type of music along with comedy and spoken word, so much from every genre. If you can find me someone who can’t find something they don’t like, then I’m going to say they don’t like music! This is a weekend for music fans.”
Everywhere At Once runs across the UK until Sunday 28 June. Visit here for events and all information.
The post Missing Glastonbury? There’s a festival on your doorstep that’s saving grassroots venues appeared first on NME.
